question.text: Which of the following asset classes is known for providing the best liquidity (ease of converting to cash)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Liquidity means how quickly and easily you can convert an asset into cash without significant loss in value. A bank savings account is the most liquid — you can withdraw money instantly using an ATM or bank branch. Real estate takes weeks or months to sell, gold jewellery involves making charges loss, and breaking an FD early may result in penalty. **The Trap:** Gold and real estate have value but are NOT liquid — selling them takes time and may involve losses. Even a 5-year FD has a premature withdrawal penalty. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under asset characteristics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Retired clients need liquidity for emergencies. Advise them to keep 3-6 months of expenses in a savings account or liquid fund before investing the rest in longer-term products.
